stories.headline: Kenya fuel prices rise sharply despite reduction in tax due to Iran war
stories.summary: Diesel prices rise by a record margin despite a reduction in value added tax.

stories.headline: Deadly Israeli strikes hit Gaza’s Al‑Shati camp and Al‑Shifa Hospital
stories.summary: Deadly Israeli strikes hit Gaza’s Al‑Shati camp and Al‑Shifa Hospital area despite a ceasefire, with at least 11 Palestinians killed in renewed violence.

stories.headline: Iran says $270bn war loss must be compensated, as fresh talks with US loom
stories.summary: Iran demands compensation for war damages caused by US and Israeli attacks on critical infrastructure.

stories.headline: US military says blockade of Iran ports ‘completely’ halts economic trade
stories.summary: US Central Command say more than 10,000 armed forces members, as well as warships and planes, are involved in blockade.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran war 'very close to over' as truce talks advance
stories.summary: A second round of talks between Washington and Tehran could take place within days, according to Trump. Meanwhile, Israel continues to press ahead with its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.

stories.headline: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.summary: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.headline: Iran faces halt to oil production within weeks if US blockade succeeds
stories.summary: Tehran has a maximum of 16 days of storage before it will need to curtail its output

stories.headline: Starmer 'not going to yield' to pressure from Trump on Iran war
stories.summary: It follows a warning from the US president that America's trade deal with the UK "can always be changed".

stories.headline: US, Iran Seek Further Talks as Hormuz Standoff Intensifies
stories.summary: The US and Iran are closer to extending a ceasefire and restarting negotiations about a longer-term peace deal, the Associated Press reported, even as a standoff intensifies over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: North Korea rapidly expanding nuclear weapons capability, UN watchdog warns
stories.summary: Pyongyang making ‘very serious’ progress on producing weapons, with rapid rise in activity at main nuclear complexNorth Korea has made “very serious” progress in its ability to produce more nuclear weapons, the head of the UN’s nuclear watchdog has said, in another sign that the regime is seeking to use its nuclear arsenal to ensure its survival.North Korea is thought to have assembled about 50 nuclear warheads, although some experts are sceptica

stories.headline: North Korea boosting ability to manufacture nuclear arms, IAEA chief warns
stories.summary: The nuclear watchdog's chief says there is a rapid increase in operations at North Korea's Yongbyon reactor.

stories.headline: Trump turns on Meloni, saying she lacks ‘courage’ over US-Israel war on Iran
stories.summary: The US president says he is 'shocked at her', delivering a blunt public rebuke to one of his closest European allies.

stories.headline: 'Trump needs a better deal than Obama', says former US envoy for Iran
stories.summary: Elliott Abrams, former US Special Representative for Iran, said in an exclusive interview with Europe Today that Benjamin Netanyahu “will follow” whatever US President Donald Trump decides regarding the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Iran used Chinese spy satellite to target US bases
stories.summary: Leaked documents show IRGC secretly acquired system and used it to guide strikes during war in March

stories.headline: European airports warn new border system causes up to three-hour delays
stories.summary: Operators in 15 countries report disruption as new EU electronic entry/exit system fully comes into force

stories.headline: EU to Give Members Leeway on Fuel, Fertilizer Prices Amid War
stories.summary: The European Union will allow member states more flexibility to subsidize fuel and fertilizer prices to soften the impact of the price shock from the Iran war.

stories.headline: Private healthcare in the UK: helping or harming the NHS?
stories.summary: Critics worry the country is heading towards a two-tier system. Dentistry is a cautionary tale

stories.headline: ‘Bifs’ replace ‘Piigs’ as Europe’s bond market whipping boys
stories.summary: Britain, Italy and France have borne the brunt of a sovereign debt sell-off sparked by the Iran war

stories.headline: Gas Spending Jumped 16% for BofA Customers Amid the War in Iran
stories.summary: Bank of America Corp. saw its customers’ spending on gas soar 16% in March as the war in Iran led to a jump in oil prices.

stories.headline: Europe’s Once-Hot Luxury Stocks Have Shed $180 Billion in 2026
stories.summary: One of Europe’s stock market pillars is crumbling as a selloff in its world-leading luxury sector deepens on signs that the Middle East war will delay a recovery in high-end spending.

stories.headline: JD Vance Heckled In Antiwar Protest at Turning Point USA Event
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance appeared to express sympathy with critics of the war with Iran: “I recognize that young voters do not love the policy we have in the Middle East, OK. I understand.”

stories.headline: Swalwell’s Exit Injects ‘Chaos’ Into California Governor’s Race
stories.summary: Democratic candidates see sudden voter interest in the sleepy contest as a campaign opportunity. All are scrambling for support from former backers of Eric Swalwell.

stories.headline: Labor lashes Coalition immigration plan as Usman Khawaja calls policy ‘appalling’
stories.summary: Burke says Taylor chasing votes lost to One Nation while cricket great urges opposition to admit policy would discriminate against MuslimsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAngus Taylor’s plans to favour immigration from liberal democracies would fundamentally change the character of the nation, Labor has warned, challenging the Coalition to explain where existing deportation powers are insufficient.Home affairs minister T

stories.headline: Japan's Cybozu bets on Malaysia, Thailand for software growth, says CEO
China's more-than-decade-late sanctions ineffective as leverage on Japan
EU cracks down on Chinese goods bypassing tariffs via Belt and Road Initiative
stories.summary: The European Commission has targeted glass fibre produced by Chinese firms using the Belt and Road initiative to bypass EU trade defences. However industry and unions warn the measures fall short and imports may continue to rise.

stories.headline: Magyar calls for fast handover of power in talks with Hungary's president
stories.summary: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

stories.headline: Kanye West postpones France gig following UK ban
stories.summary: It comes a week after the UK government announced it would block the rapper from entering the country.

stories.headline: Should Europe welcome Chinese investment?
stories.summary: Brussels wants firms opening factories in the EU to transfer knowledge and employ local staff. Will it work?

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley Stock Traders Join in Wall Street’s Windfall
stories.summary: Morgan Stanley’s stock traders joined the rest of Wall Street with a record-breaking first quarter.

stories.headline: Brazil Taps Euro Bond Market for First Time in Over a Decade
stories.summary: Brazil is returning to international debt markets with its first sale of euro-denominated bonds in over a decade, joining a wave of emerging-market borrowers seeking to diversify their funding sources.

stories.headline: JPMorgan Starts Marketing New Bonds After Earnings Release
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is offering investment-grade bonds in as many as four parts, following the financial heavyweight’s record trading haul in the first quarter.

stories.headline: South Korea Overtook China as ASML’s Largest Market Last Quarter
stories.summary: South Korea emerged as ASML Holding NV’s largest market in the first quarter as the country’s memory chip makers increase purchases to address chronic shortages stemming from the artificial intelligence boom.

stories.headline: Silver Market Poised for Sixth Straight Annual Deficit This Year
stories.summary: The global silver market is expected to remain in a deficit for a sixth consecutive year amid robust demand for bars and coins, and declining supplies, according to the Silver Institute.

stories.headline: Hedge Funds Built for Crypto Turn to Oil and Gold on 24/7 Venues
stories.summary: For years, crypto hedge funds operated inside a marketplace most of Wall Street never saw — trading tokens around the clock on platforms with no closing bell, no clearing house, and no regulatory oversight. The strategies were exotic, the returns were volatile, and the entire operation ran on infrastructure that never slept.

stories.headline: US Premarket Movers: BRP, Cloudflare, Robinhood, Snap, Webull
stories.summary: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:46 a.m. in New York after a rally that took stocks near all-time highs on Tuesday.

stories.headline: Adani Unit Refinances Offshore Bond with Apollo-Backed Funds
stories.summary: A unit of the Adani Group has refinanced its offshore bond maturing in June using funds from a $500 million private placement last month, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Abu Dhabi’s XRG Upbeat on European Prospects, Open to More M&A
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi’s state oil company, which recently closed a three-way merger to create a $60 billion petrochemical behemoth, said it’s confident about Europe’s economic prospects and remains open to new deals.

stories.headline: Air New Zealand's economy Skynest bunk beds set for launch
stories.summary: Passengers can book a four-hour session in the bunk beds from May for Auckland-New York flights but airline cautions against smuggling in childrenEconomy passengers on Air New Zealand’s ultra-long-haul flight between Auckland and New York can book a spot in the airline’s bunk-bed style sleeping pods from May, which will take to skies in late 2026.In what the airline says is a world first, six full-length, lie-flat sleeping pods, are squeezed into

stories.headline: Victoria left without limits on political donations after high court rules laws unconstitutional
stories.summary: Scrapping a law which left a loophole for Labor and Coalition funding will expose the state to ‘dark money from foreign billionaires’, says premierFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe high court has ruled Victoria’s political donation laws are unconstitutional, leaving the state with no limits on donations and no disclosure requirements unless new legislation is urgen

stories.headline: Australian lesbian group’s fight to bar trans women to return to tribunal after federal court win
stories.summary: While the Lesbian Action Group claims a ‘definite win’, Equality Australia says the judge ‘simply identified legal errors in the tribunal’s reasoning’Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA Victorian lesbian group has won a legal appeal in its case to exclude transgender women from its public events after the federal court set aside a decision by the Australian Human Rights Commission (AHRC).The decision on Wednesday afternoo

stories.headline: Candidate calls for new election in Peru, claiming fraud without evidence
stories.summary: Peru’s presidential candidate Rafael Lopez Aliaga is urging officials to declare Sunday’s election 'null and void’.

stories.headline: Suzuki Motor, after India success, sets sights on 10% of African market
Tokyo DisneySea celebrates 25 years as foreigners increasingly fuel growth
Auntie AI: China's chatbot makers cultivate senior users
Japan to ban in-flight use of portable power banks from this month
Invesco to enter Japan ETF market amid deregulation push
China set new records in trade and investment in Central Asia in 2025
stories.summary: China's trade with five Central Asian countries hit a record in 2025 — and the relationship has moved well beyond buying and selling. Chinese companies are now partners in strategic projects across high-tech industries including energy and mining.

stories.headline: Is the European Union reliant on trade with China?
stories.summary: The European Union is struggling to contain a ballooning trade deficit with China, but member states cannot agree on how to push back.

stories.headline: Magyar vows to shut down Hungarian state TV accusing it of 'North Korean' propaganda
stories.summary: Magyar has threatened to suspend the state broadcaster following a heated on-air clash, accusing it of spreading “North Korean-style” propaganda and false claims about his family during the election campaign.

stories.headline: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
stories.summary: The official tells the BBC that the armed group will not move forward with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

stories.headline: US blockade of Iran ports irresponsible and dangerous, China says
stories.summary: China, the biggest buyer of Iranian oil, says the US naval blockade undermines an "already fragile ceasefire".

stories.headline: Oil Holds Loss on Prospect of Fresh Peace Talks With Hormuz Shut
stories.summary: Oil largely held onto a sharp drop from this week’s highs as the US and Iran seek further talks to end a war that has brought the vital Strait of Hormuz waterway to a near-halt.

stories.headline: Watch: What are Harry and Meghan doing in Australia?
stories.summary: BBC News journalist Simon Atkinson explains how the couple's tour will be different compared to their last visit.

stories.headline: Italy suspends defence agreement with Israel
stories.summary: The Italian PM said the pact, which happens every five years, would be suspended "in view of the current situation".

stories.headline: Traders Target Bond Market Rally on Iran War ‘Tone Change’
stories.summary: Optimism about a possible peace deal in the Middle East and lower oil prices has bond traders positioning for gains in Treasuries that could send 10-year yields sliding toward 4%.

stories.headline: Justice Dept. Moves to Vacate Jan. 6 Convictions for Far-Right Extremists
stories.summary: Defending the convictions would likely have required administration officials to assert that far-right groups were acting on behalf of President Trump on Jan. 6, 2021.

stories.headline: Resignations of Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales Set Up Special Election Fights in Texas and California
stories.summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California called a special election to replace Mr. Swalwell, while Gov. Greg Abbott of Texas had yet to announce one for Mr. Gonzales’s successor.

stories.headline: A New Accuser Says Eric Swalwell Sexually Assaulted Her
stories.summary: The woman said Mr. Swalwell, who resigned from Congress on Tuesday afternoon, raped her in a West Hollywood hotel room in 2018. She said she believed she was drugged.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Accuses Biden DOJ of Unfairly Prosecuting Anti-Abortion Activists
stories.summary: The report is part of the president’s effort to claim anti-conservative and anti-Christian biases in federal law enforcement, even as he pushes to wield the legal system against his political enemies.

stories.headline: DOJ moves to undo Jan. 6 rioters’ convictions for seditious conspiracy
stories.summary: President Donald Trump last year commuted the prison sentences of 12 members of the Proud Boys and Oath Keepers.

stories.headline: What Swalwell’s exit means for Democrats in California’s governor race
stories.summary: Billionaire Tom Steyer and ex-congresswoman Katie Porter stand to benefit the most in a Democratic field with no clear front-runner, several strategists said.

stories.headline: JD Vance defends backing 'great guy' Orbán's campaign after landslide defeat
stories.summary: Hungary's leader was a "great guy" who did a "very good job", the US vice-president said, but he was sure he could work with Péter Magyar.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan mix charity and business on Australia visit
stories.summary: There are questions over whether local taxpayers are picking up any of the bill for policing the visit.

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.headline: US Stocks Hold Near All-Time High on Peace Hopes: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US stocks are holding firm near an all-time high following a rally powered by optimism around a potential peace deal in the Middle East, with traders now eyeing confirmation of new talks.

stories.headline: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.summary: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
stories.summary: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.headline: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Jesus-like image and feud with the Pope are sparking backlash
stories.summary: BBC's Sarah Smith looks at how the president's most ardent supporters are reacting to his latest social media posts.

stories.headline: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.headline: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
stories.summary: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.
